title: Gluten-Free Mediterranean Falafel Bowl
description: A vibrant Mediterranean-inspired bowl featuring crispy baked falafels made with chickpeas, hemp seeds, and aromatic herbs. Served over fresh greens with cucumber, tomatoes, kalamata olives, feta cheese, and a creamy herbed yogurt sauce. This nutritious, grain-free meal is naturally gluten-free and bursting with Mediterranean flavors.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons packed parsley (flat leaf or curly)
4 baby carrots (or equivalent portion of a full-size carrot)
1 scallion, white and green parts, cut into 4-5 pieces
1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Spray olive or avocado oil (for baking sheet)
1-2 cups mixed greens or lettuce of choice
1/2 cup cucumbers, quartered, skin left on
1-2 tablespoons feta cheese or vegan feta, to taste
Balsamic vinegar and olive oil for drizzling, to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and lightly spray with olive or avocado oil.

Process the aromatics in a food processor by pulsing the 2 tablespoons parsley, 2 tablespoons cilantro, 4 baby carrots, 1 scallion (cut into pieces), and 1 clove garlic until finely chopped, scraping down the sides as needed.

Add the remaining falafel ingredients to the food processor: drained and rinsed chickpeas, 2 tablespoons chickpea flour, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1 1/2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon hemp seeds, 1/2 tablespoon chia seeds, 1 teaspoon ground flax seeds, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Pulse until well combined but still textured — do NOT purée into a smooth paste.
Rest the falafel mixture for 5 minutes to allow the chia and flax seeds to absorb moisture and act as binders.

Shape the mixture using a spoon or small cookie scoop (about 2 tablespoons each) to form approximately 9 balls, then gently flatten into thick patties about 3/4 inch thick. Place on the prepared baking sheet.
Spray the tops of the falafels lightly with olive or avocado oil spray for crispiness.
Bake at 375°F for 15 minutes. Flip carefully, spray again lightly, and bake for an additional 12-15 minutes until golden brown and crispy on the outside.

Make the herbed yogurt sauce by stirring together 1 cup Greek yogurt, 3-4 teaspoons lemon juice, 1 1/2 tablespoons chopped dill, 1 clove minced garlic, 1 tablespoon chopped parsley, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl. Taste and adjust seasonings as desired. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Divide the mixed greens among 3 bowls. Top each with cucumbers, cherry tomatoes, red onion, kalamata olives, and feta cheese.
Add 3 tablespoons of hummus and 3 baked falafels to each bowl.
Drizzle with herbed yogurt sauce, then finish with a light drizzle of balsamic vinegar and olive oil.
notes: For gluten-free: This recipe is naturally gluten-free using chickpea flour. Can substitute gluten-free all-purpose flour or gluten-free oat flour for chickpea flour if needed.
For vegan: Use Greek almond milk yogurt for the sauce and vegan feta to make the entire bowl vegan.

Make-ahead: Falafel mixture can be made a day ahead and refrigerated. Recipe doubles well — freeze extra baked falafels on a sheet pan, then transfer to a bag. Reheat from frozen at 375°F for 10-12 minutes.
Don't over-process the chickpeas. Pulse in short bursts; the mixture should look like coarse breadcrumbs that hold together when squeezed.
Drain chickpeas thoroughly and pat them dry with a towel after rinsing. Excess moisture makes soggy falafels that won't hold together.
